Application version is defined.functional: Application reports a valid version string so users and logs can identify the running version
Application version is defined.technical: CortexVision.version returns a non-empty string in semantic version format (X.Y.Z)
Application version is defined.expectedOutput: Non-empty string containing at least one dot (e.g. "0.1.0")

All capture modes have a non-empty label.functional: Each capture mode displays a descriptive label in the toolbar
All capture modes have a non-empty label.technical: CaptureMode.allCases each return a non-empty label string

All capture modes have an SF Symbol.functional: Each capture mode displays an icon in the toolbar using SF Symbols
All capture modes have an SF Symbol.technical: CaptureMode.allCases each return a valid SF Symbol name string

There are exactly three capture modes.functional: The app offers exactly three capture modes: window, region, scrolling

Each mode has a unique identifier.functional: Toolbar can distinguish between modes for correct selection state
Each mode has a unique identifier.technical: CaptureMode.id values are unique across all cases

Idle state shows no capture message.functional: Status bar shows 'No capture' when app has not captured anything

Capturing state shows progress message.functional: Status bar indicates capture is in progress

Captured state shows dimensions.functional: After capture, status bar shows image dimensions

Analyzed state shows word and figure cou.functional: After analysis, status bar shows word and figure counts
Analyzed state shows word and figure cou.technical: CaptureState.analyzed.statusText contains word and figure counts

Error state shows error message.functional: Status bar shows error description when something goes wrong

CaptureProvider protocol is defined and.functional: System can accept any capture implementation via protocol abstraction
CaptureProvider protocol is defined and.technical: CaptureProvider protocol compiles and can be referenced as existential type

ScrollCaptureProvider protocol is define.functional: System can accept any scroll capture implementation via protocol abstraction
ScrollCaptureProvider protocol is define.technical: ScrollCaptureProvider protocol compiles and can be referenced

ExportDestination protocol is defined an.functional: System can accept any export implementation via protocol abstraction
ExportDestination protocol is defined an.technical: ExportDestination protocol compiles and can be referenced

PermissionManager protocol is defined an.functional: System can accept any permission manager implementation via protocol
PermissionManager protocol is defined an.technical: PermissionManager protocol compiles and can be referenced

CaptureResult can be constructed with re.functional: A capture result carries the image, target and timestamp
CaptureResult can be constructed with re.technical: CaptureResult init accepts CGImage, CaptureTarget, Date

ExportConfiguration generates correct fi.functional: Export knows where to write markdown and figure files
ExportConfiguration generates correct fi.technical: ExportConfiguration.markdownURL and figuresDirectoryURL are derived correctly
ExportConfiguration generates correct fi.expectedOutput: markdownURL ends with capture.md, figuresDirectoryURL ends with figures

ScrollCaptureProgress reports estimated.functional: During scrolling capture, user sees progress percentage

ScrollCaptureProgress handles unknown to.functional: Progress is nil when total frames is unknown during scrolling capture
ScrollCaptureProgress handles unknown to.technical: ScrollCaptureProgress.estimatedProgress returns nil when total is nil

PermissionStatus has all expected cases.functional: System can represent all possible OS permission states
PermissionStatus has all expected cases.technical: PermissionStatus has granted, denied, notDetermined, restricted cases

ScreenCaptureKitProvider conforms to Cap.functional: System can use ScreenCaptureKit as a capture provider
ScreenCaptureKitProvider conforms to Cap.technical: ScreenCaptureKitProvider compiles and satisfies CaptureProvider protocol
ScreenCaptureKitProvider conforms to Cap.expectedOutput: Instance assignable to any CaptureProvider variable

LocalPermissionManager conforms to Permi.functional: System can manage permissions via LocalPermissionManager
LocalPermissionManager conforms to Permi.technical: LocalPermissionManager compiles and satisfies PermissionManager protocol
LocalPermissionManager conforms to Permi.expectedOutput: Instance assignable to any PermissionManager variable

CaptureError provides descriptive messag.functional: User sees clear error messages when capture fails
CaptureError provides descriptive messag.expectedOutput: Each case has a non-empty localizedDescription

CaptureError.permissionDenied mentions S.functional: Permission error guides user to System Settings

CaptureState transitions to error on per.functional: Status bar shows permission error when capture fails due to permissions
CaptureState transitions to error on per.technical: CaptureState.error with CaptureError.permissionDenied message shows correct text

RegionSelector can be instantiated.technical: RegionSelector can be created without parameters

RegionSelector cancel calls completion w.functional: ESC during region selection cancels and returns to idle
RegionSelector cancel calls completion w.technical: RegionSelector.cancel() calls completion handler with nil CGRect

flipAndClampRect flips Y-axis from AppKi.functional: Region selection coordinates are correctly translated for capture
flipAndClampRect flips Y-axis from AppKi.technical: flipAndClampRect converts AppKit origin (bottom-left) to CG origin (top-left)

flipAndClampRect handles origin at botto.functional: Region at the bottom of the screen maps to top in CG coords
flipAndClampRect handles origin at botto.technical: AppKit y=0 (bottom) flips to y=displayHeight-height (top) in CG

flipAndClampRect clamps rect that extend.functional: A selection that partially extends beyond the screen is clipped
flipAndClampRect clamps rect that extend.technical: Rect extending past display edge is intersected with display bounds

flipAndClampRect returns nil for fully o.functional: A selection completely outside the screen is rejected
flipAndClampRect returns nil for fully o.technical: flipAndClampRect returns nil when rect has no overlap with display

flipAndClampRect handles zero-size displ.functional: No crash on edge case with zero-size display

flipAndClampRect preserves full-screen r.functional: Selecting the entire screen produces valid coordinates
flipAndClampRect preserves full-screen r.technical: Full-screen AppKit rect flips to (0,0) in CG with same dimensions

flipAndClampRect handles rect on seconda.functional: Region capture works on secondary screen with display offset
flipAndClampRect handles rect on seconda.technical: Rect relative to a secondary display flips Y correctly within that display's bounds

flipAndClampRect clamps rect extending b.functional: Region at top edge of screen is clipped correctly
flipAndClampRect clamps rect extending b.technical: AppKit rect extending beyond top of display is clamped after Y-flip

LocalPermissionManager screenRecordingSt.functional: App can determine current screen recording permission state
LocalPermissionManager screenRecordingSt.expectedOutput: One of the expected PermissionStatus values

LocalPermissionManager requestScreenReco.functional: Permission request completes and returns a definitive answer
LocalPermissionManager requestScreenReco.technical: requestScreenRecording() returns true when permission is already granted

availableWindows returns non-empty list.functional: User sees a list of available windows to capture
availableWindows returns non-empty list.technical: ScreenCaptureKitProvider.availableWindows() queries SCShareableContent and returns WindowInfo items with title, appName, and frame
availableWindows returns non-empty list.input: Live system with at least one visible window
availableWindows returns non-empty list.expectedOutput: Non-empty [WindowInfo] with valid titles and positive frame dimensions

captureRegion captures a valid image.functional: User selects a screen region and gets a capture
captureRegion captures a valid image.technical: ScreenCaptureKitProvider.captureRegion creates SCContentFilter + SCStreamConfiguration, captures via SCScreenshotManager, returns CGImage at retina resolution
captureRegion captures a valid image.input: CGRect(x: 100, y: 100, width: 500, height: 300) in AppKit screen coordinates
captureRegion captures a valid image.expectedOutput: CGImage with positive width/height, CaptureResult.target == .region with matching rect

captured image contains recognizable con.functional: Captured screen region contains text that OCR can read
captured image contains recognizable con.technical: Capture menu bar area via captureRegion, run VNRecognizeTextRequest with .accurate level, verify non-empty observations
captured image contains recognizable con.input: Region capture of menu bar area (0, screenHeight-30, 600, 30) in AppKit coords
captured image contains recognizable con.expectedOutput: At least one VNRecognizedTextObservation (Apple menu, app name, clock, etc.)

AnalysisOverlayView renders without cras.functional: Preview overlay shows nothing when no analysis results exist
AnalysisOverlayView renders without cras.technical: AnalysisOverlay array is empty, pixelRect computation handles gracefully
AnalysisOverlayView renders without cras.expectedOutput: No crash, no overlay rects produced

pixelRect correctly converts normalized.functional: Overlay bounding boxes appear at correct positions on the preview image
pixelRect correctly converts normalized.technical: AnalysisOverlay.pixelRect(for:) converts 0..1 bounds to pixel coords

pixelRect for figure overlay at differen.functional: Figure bounding boxes are correctly positioned

Text overlay has correct kind.functional: Text annotations are visually distinct from figure annotations
Text overlay has correct kind.technical: AnalysisOverlay with .text kind reports correct kind

Figure overlay has correct kind.functional: Figure annotations are visually distinct from text annotations
Figure overlay has correct kind.technical: AnalysisOverlay with .figure kind reports correct kind

pixelRect handles zero-size image gracef.functional: Overlay computation does not crash on edge cases
pixelRect handles zero-size image gracef.technical: pixelRect with zero imageSize returns zero rect

Each overlay has a unique identifier.functional: Multiple overlays can be distinguished for rendering
Each overlay has a unique identifier.technical: Default UUID generation produces unique ids
Each overlay has a unique identifier.input: Two overlays created with default id

Window capture contains correct QR code.functional: A captured window contains all its visual content, verifiable by QR decode
Window capture contains correct QR code.technical: Create reference window with CIQRCodeGenerator QR encoding a UUID, capture via ScreenCaptureKitProvider.captureWindow, decode with VNDetectBarcodesRequest, compare payload to original UUID
Window capture contains correct QR code.input: NSWindow (400x400, .floating) with QR code encoding UUID, captured by window ID match
Window capture contains correct QR code.expectedOutput: VNDetectBarcodesRequest decodes QR payload identical to the generated UUID

Region capture contains correct QR code.functional: A captured screen region contains the exact content at that position
Region capture contains correct QR code.technical: Create reference window at known screen position, capture that region via captureRegion(window.frame), decode QR with VNDetectBarcodesRequest, compare to original UUID
Region capture contains correct QR code.input: NSWindow (400x400, .floating, center of primary screen), region capture of window.frame
Region capture contains correct QR code.expectedOutput: VNDetectBarcodesRequest decodes QR payload identical to the generated UUID

Region capture has colored corners in co.functional: Captured image is not cropped or shifted — all four corners are present and correctly positioned
Region capture has colored corners in co.technical: Create reference window with 40pt colored corners (red TL, green TR, blue BL, yellow BR), capture content rect via captureRegion, sample pixels at 10pt inset from each corner using scale-aware coordinates, compare RGB values with tolerance 80 for macOS P3 color space
Region capture has colored corners in co.input: NSWindow (400x400) with colored corners, region capture of contentLayoutRect in screen coords
Region capture has colored corners in co.expectedOutput: Top-left pixel near (255,0,0), top-right near (0,255,0), bottom-left near (0,0,255), bottom-right near (255,255,0) within tolerance 80

Region capture contains recognizable tex.functional: Captured region text is readable by OCR — full content integrity
Region capture contains recognizable tex.technical: Create reference window with known label 'CortexVision Verify' (16pt bold), capture region via captureRegion(window.frame), run VNRecognizeTextRequest with .accurate level, verify recognized text contains expected words
Region capture contains recognizable tex.input: NSWindow (400x400, .floating) with text label 'CortexVision Verify', region capture of window.frame
Region capture contains recognizable tex.expectedOutput: Joined OCR candidates contain both 'CortexVision' and 'Verify'

Secondary screen capture contains correc.functional: Capture works correctly on a secondary display with different coordinate offset
Secondary screen capture contains correc.technical: Create reference window on secondary NSScreen, capture region via captureRegion(window.frame) which must correctly match NSScreen to SCDisplay and convert coordinates, decode QR with VNDetectBarcodesRequest
Secondary screen capture contains correc.input: NSWindow (400x400, .floating) centered on secondary screen, region capture of window.frame
Secondary screen capture contains correc.expectedOutput: VNDetectBarcodesRequest decodes QR payload identical to the generated UUID
